data.summary: Auto-ack research: in-session re-dump already solved by WP-11b suppression; the real bloat is CROSS-SESSION — last_seen is in-memory only, so a new session's startup drain re-dumps every read-but-unacked body in full. Colton confirms agents routinely don't ack across long agentic runs, so N>0 is the common path.
data.detail:
Investigated task 156f27bb476a (Colton's auto-ack idea). Findings:

(a) Two regimes. IN-SESSION: WP-11b body suppression (tools.py:684-732) already prevents re-dumping a read-but-unacked message — it renders only a ~20-tok 'already read this session, not re-shown' note. CROSS-SESSION: last_seen lives only in Identity._last_seen (server.py:67), reset to None on every new session/server-restart/name-change. On a new session the watcher baselines pre-existing unread (no wake, channel.py:274-277) and the standing startup teammate_inbox drain re-dumps every lingering read-but-unacked message in FULL (_prev_seen empty). THAT is the token bloat.

(b) last_seen IS NONE is a load-bearing SENTINEL with two non-suppression consumers: ack('all') startup-drain (tools.py:755-758) and the watcher's unseen filter (channel.py:285-286). So naively PERSISTING last_seen would silently turn startup ack('all') into a selective no-op. (Correction from peer review: I had also claimed it feeds the watcher's known_ids seeding — false; known_ids is a watcher-local var, independent of identity.last_seen. The real watcher coupling is the unseen filter, and that filter still gets populated by the in-memory set_last_seen call which Approach A leaves intact, so the watcher is unaffected.)

(c) RECOMMENDED FIX (Approach A): a SEPARATE persisted shown-set keyed by agent name (e.g. {agent}_seen.json), consulted ONLY for body suppression in _handle_inbox, leaving the in-memory last_seen=None sentinel untouched. Preserves NEVER-MISS, ack/reply affordance, ack('all') startup-drain, read-receipts (group_read_positions reads _read.json), and the watcher no-noise/re-nudge contract. Rejected full auto-ack-on-read as default: changes read-receipt timing (fires on read not ack) and removes the deliberate leave-unacked affordance for marginal extra savings.

(d) Sizing: initially looked marginal (fires once per session start, only for deliberately-unacked msgs). Colton's real-world signal flips this: agents often DON'T ack across long agentic tasks + multiple compactions, so the unacked backlog is common and the re-dump is frequent → solid default-on value.

Peer review (sonnet) verdict SOUND-WITH-CHANGES. Three implementation requirements for the WP: (D2) prune the persisted set to current-unread at LOAD time, not lazily on first use, to close a stale-id window; (D3) the count-line copy 'already read this session' (tools.py:706,731) becomes a lie cross-session and must be reworded; (D4 resolved by Colton's signal). High-water-mark cursor is simpler but breaks under windowed since/limit reads (WP-7), so the SET is the correct structure.
data.author: Silvie

data.summary: WP-15 brief — Durable cross-session inbox body-suppression
data.detail:
# WP-15 — Durable cross-session inbox body-suppression

Manager: Silvie (planner, does not implement). Implementer: Svetlana. Branch: feat/wp15-cross-session-suppression. Target v0.11.0. Source task 156f27bb476a. Research discovery 9fb4d08007ed.

PROBLEM: teammate_inbox body-suppression (WP-11b) prevents re-dumping a read-but-unacked message body WITHIN a session, but the seen-set lives only in Identity._last_seen (in-memory, server.py:67), lost on every new session/server restart/re-register. So on the first teammate_inbox of a new session the startup drain re-dumps the full body of every lingering read-but-unacked message. Agents routinely leave messages unacked across long agentic runs and compactions (Colton-confirmed), so this is the common path.

FIX (Approach A): add a per-agent persisted shown-set {agent}_seen.json in the inboxes dir, consulted ONLY for body suppression in _handle_inbox. Do NOT persist Identity._last_seen itself (load-bearing sentinel for ack-all startup-drain at tools.py:755-758 and the watcher unseen filter at channel.py:285-286). Steps: (0) seen_file = get_inboxes_dir/f'{agent}_seen.json'; read_json_safe returns [] if missing. (1) load+prune AFTER count_only early return (tools.py:661): persisted = set(read_json_safe(seen_file)) & _unread_ids, pruned at load time. (2) suppression set _prev_seen = in-mem last_seen ∪ persisted, used only for new_msgs/seen_msgs split (tools.py:698-699); brand-new id in neither → renders full (NEVER-MISS). (3) set_last_seen line unchanged but semantics shift (now absorbs prior-session ids) — intentional. (4) persist immediately after set_last_seen (tools.py:687), before any early return: write_json_atomic(seen_file, sorted((persisted ∪ _shown) & _unread_ids)). (5) reword count-line copy at tools.py:704,706,731 (all say 'this session', all false cross-session); include sender names for post-compaction triage.

CONCURRENCY: write_json_atomic prevents torn writes; lock-free read-modify-write can lose an update o

data.summary: WP-15 shipped (v0.11.0): durable cross-session inbox body-suppression via per-agent {agent}_seen.json; 8 acceptance tests, gated by manager revert-run, T5 tautology caught in re-gate
data.detail:
WP-15 makes teammate_inbox body-suppression durable across sessions. Problem: WP-11b suppression lived only in Identity._last_seen (in-memory), so the first inbox read of a new session re-dumped the full body of every read-but-unacked message (the common path — Colton confirmed agents routinely don't ack across long agentic runs/compactions). Fix (Approach A): a separate persisted per-agent shown-set, {agent}_seen.json in the inboxes dir, consulted ONLY for the new/seen split in _handle_inbox; _prev_seen = (in-memory last_seen ∪ persisted). Identity._last_seen=None sentinel left untouched (still drives ack-all startup-drain + watcher unseen filter). Load-time prune (& _unread_ids) keeps the file bounded and prevents stale-id resurrection; no tail-cap. Count-line reworded to name senders ("N already delivered (from: Alice×2, Bob)") as the post-compaction triage affordance. show_all=True still re-dumps. Implementer: Svetlana. Manager/gate: Silvie.

Gate: full harness green on Windows at da19f1f, then again at 3a82a34; manager ran the revert himself (persisted=set()) in an isolated worktree — T1/T2/T4/T8 went RED with the correct reason (bodies re-dump), T3/T5/T6/T7 behavior as expected. Adversarial subagent review = SOUND-WITH-CHANGES: flagged T5 as tautological (show_all bypasses suppression, so it can't distinguish feature-on from feature-off). Re-gate at 3a82a34 fixed T5 (assert suppressed-without / shown-with in same session) — verified T5 now goes RED on revert. Zero new deps, stdlib-only.
data.author: Silvie

data.summary: WP-15: separate persisted {agent}_seen.json for body-suppression (Approach A). Rejected persisting Identity._last_seen (breaks ack-all/watcher None sentinel) and full auto-ack (changes receipt timing + drops the leave-unacked affordance)
data.detail:
Chosen: add a per-agent {agent}_seen.json (JSON list of shown message ids) consulted ONLY for the new_msgs/seen_msgs split in _handle_inbox. _prev_seen = (in-memory last_seen ∪ persisted), captured BEFORE set_last_seen so NEVER-MISS holds. Pruned to & _unread_ids on load; persisted after set_last_seen before early returns; write is atomic but NOT lock-held (lost-update tolerated — MCP calls per agent are serialized; do NOT add a lock).

Rejected — persist Identity._last_seen itself: last_seen=None is a load-bearing SENTINEL meaning "never read this session", consumed by ack("all") startup-drain (tools.py:755-758) and the watcher unseen filter (channel.py:285-286). Persisting it non-None would make ack-all drain only prior-session ids and orphan fresh arrivals. The whole point of a separate seen_file is to leave the sentinel alone.

Rejected — full auto-ack-on-read as default: gives honest unread counts but changes read-receipt timing (receipts fire on read not ack), removes the deliberate leave-unacked-as-TODO affordance, and is a bigger protocol-surface change. Deferred to its own WP if inbox-never-drains becomes a felt problem.

Deliberate tradeoff: after a compaction the agent sees only the count-line for bodies it no longer holds — mitigated by naming senders in the count-line (triage), with show_all=True as the recovery hatch.
data.author: Silvie

data.summary: WP-15 re-gate: manager's own revert-run + adversarial subagent caught 3 tautological tests (T5/T6/T7) that a handed green hid — T5 (show_all bypasses suppression) passed with the feature reverted. Reinforces: run the revert, don't reason about it
data.detail: At the WP-15 gate, Svetlana handed 44/44 green at da19f1f. The manager did NOT trust the green: ran the full suite in an isolated worktree, then reverted the seen_file load (persisted=set()) and re-ran. T1/T2/T4/T8 went red (genuine feature guards), but T5/T6/T7 stayed green — an adversarial sonnet reviewer independently classified T5 as tautological: show_all=True bypasses suppression entirely, so the test passes whether the feature exists or not. T6/T7 were defensible adjacent guards (set_last_seen still fires; count_only write-placement) but under-documented. Fix folded in re-gate (3a82a34): T5 rewritten to assert suppressed-without-show_all THEN shown-with-show_all in the same session, so its without-show_all half now goes red on the revert. Lesson: a passing test is not a guarding test; the revert/tautology check (assert the failure REASON, run the revert rather than reason about it) is what separates a real net from a green that proves nothing. Instance of the gate-correctness-net pattern (c074742c6570).
data.author: Silvie

data.summary: fable-audit epic (260701) — teammate-comms
data.detail: Confirmed intent (human-approved yardstick): teammate-comms is a Claude Code plugin (pure-stdlib MCP server) that lets independent, human-launched full Claude Code instances message each other and wake each other from idle — closing the gap that built-in SendMessage only covers parent→subagent. It provides identity/registration, DMs, groups, reactions, profiles, a local dashboard where the human is a first-class teammate, and opt-in "reincarnate" spawning. Primary users / audit yardstick: THIRD-PARTY ADOPTERS — a brand-new Claude Code user/team installing from the marketplace with zero context. Definition of success for a new consumer: install the plugin, register via teammate_register, and reliably send/receive/wake across two or more full instances (cross-project, cross-OS) with no polling and no ports. This epic parents all fix-tasks filed by the 260701 fable-audit (stages: systems, interconnectivity, consumer).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fable Audit 260701 — Stage 1: Per-system audit (systems)
data.detail:
Fable Audit Stage 1 (2026-07-01), teammate-comms, yardstick = third-party adopters installing from marketplace with zero context. 8 Sonnet auditors (server core, comms.py storage, tools.py, channel+spawn, dashboard+avatars, hooks/packaging/CI, tests, docs accuracy). Full text: docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md. Findings (id, severity, title):
S1 critical bug: non-dict JSON-RPC message kills server — handle() unguarded (server.py:223-252, 346-354); sibling paths (dispatch WP-5, watcher WP-12) hardened, envelope missed.
S2 high blindspot: register name-collision only logged to stderr; tool returns unconditional success (server.py:180-187, 216-220). Found independently by two auditors.
S3 medium: protocolVersion echoed not negotiated (server.py:227-237).
S4 medium: $TEAMMATE_AGENT auto-register failure silent (server.py:255-265) — reincarnate child looks alive, never registers.
S5 low: notification-vs-request inconsistent (id:null frames possible).
S6 low: "no polling loop needed" instruction overstates — deliberate, validated (097c03396bd4).
C1 high bug: teammate_inbox reads unread.json with destructive read_json_safe, unlocked (tools.py:667 vs channel.py:266) — contradicts AUDIT-v0.7.0 "verified solid"; risk of silent inbox wipe on synced/network filesystems (OneDrive ~/.claude).
C2 high bug: unbounded full-scan of global reactions.jsonl on every inbox/group-history call (tools.py:726, 1310) — WP-7 fixed the pattern elsewhere, missed hottest sites.
C3 med-high: append_group_message rewrites whole history per message (comms.py:401-420), never compacted.
C4 med-high: naive local-time message ids vs documented cross-host mode (comms.py:33-35 vs DESIGN.md:670-676) — clock skew silently skips messages past cursors.
C5 medium: global flat namespace, offline collision unguarded (deliberate root ef4af8135c03, unrevisited).
C6 medium: transcript/reactions no compaction (C-2 deferral).
T1 high bug: teammate_set_avatar can target any teammate — no caller check (tools.py:1010-1
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Guard JSON-RPC envelope: wrap per-message handling, reject non-dict/batch input gracefully
data.detail: Fable-audit S1 (critical bug). handle() assumes msg is a dict; main loop guards json.loads but not handle(msg, ctx). A bare scalar/null/JSON-RPC batch array on stdin raises uncaught AttributeError and kills the server. Fix: try/except around handle per message; respond with a JSON-RPC error (or stay silent for id-less input) instead of dying. Evidence: server.py:223-252, 346-354. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #S1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Restore test-suite integrity: wire WP-13/WP-14 suites into CI, fix C:/cctmp hardcode, un-stale version literals
data.detail: Fable-audit Q1+Q2+Q3 (critical). CI runs only test_handshake.py; test_wp13_projects.py and test_wp14_avatars.py never run anywhere. Proof of rot: test_wp13_projects.py:411 hardcodes dir="C:/cctmp" (FileNotFoundError on any other machine) and both suites assert version == literal "0.10.0" (shipped is 0.11.0). Fix: default tempfile location, replace literals with the cross-file consistency check already used in test_handshake.py:2895-2902, add both suites to ci.yml. Evidence: ci.yml:41-42, test_wp13_projects.py:411,649, test_wp14_avatars.py:441. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #Q1-Q3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: First-contact diagnostics: detect missing uv/bash and surface an actionable MCP-spawn failure
data.detail: Fable-audit P1+P3 (critical). plugin.json spawns via "command": "uv" — if uv is absent the session-start.sh warning (a disconnected mechanism) never helps and the spawn fails cryptically. If Git Bash is absent on Windows the hooks never run, venv never builds, and uv run --no-sync fails against an unsynced venv. Fix direction: existence checks + actionable in-band messaging (e.g. a wrapper entry that reports what's missing), and README prerequisites that don't assume prior plugins. Evidence: plugin.json:9-14, hooks/session-start.sh:47-50, hooks/hooks.json:9,19. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #P1,#P3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Surface identity collisions in-conversation (register live-collision, offline collision, dashboard human-name)
data.detail: Fable-audit S2+C5+D1 (high). Live name-collision at register only logs to stderr while the tool returns unconditional success (server.py:180-187, 216-220); offline cross-project collisions have no guard at all (comms.py:221-250); two dashboards defaulting to "human" silently merge identities (tools.py:1565-1569, comms.py:896-914). Fix: return collision warnings/errors in the tool result text where the agent/human will actually see them. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #S2,#C5,#D1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Inbox read safety: stop using destructive read_json_safe unlocked on unread.json in _handle_inbox
data.detail: Fable-audit C1 (high bug). teammate_inbox reads {agent}_unread.json via read_json_safe (resets file to [] on any read failure) with no lock, on the highest-contention multi-writer file; the watcher reads the same file with read_json_readonly. On non-atomic-rename filesystems (network shares, OneDrive-synced ~/.claude) a transient failure silently wipes a live inbox. Also contradicts AUDIT-v0.7.0.md "verified solid" claim. Fix: read_json_readonly and/or lock. Evidence: tools.py:667 vs channel.py:266; comms.py:440-465. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #C1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Bound reactions reads: pass limit at the inbox and group-history read_reactions call sites
data.detail: Fable-audit C2 (high bug). _handle_inbox (tools.py:726) and group history (tools.py:1310) call read_reactions with no limit, bypassing the tail-read fast path (comms.py:1242-1268) and parsing the entire global cross-project reactions.jsonl on every call — every inbox call on the machine gets linearly slower forever. WP-7 fixed this pattern in react()/resolve_message() but missed these hottest call sites. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #C2.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Restrict teammate_set_avatar target to the caller (or human operator)
data.detail: Fable-audit T1 (high bug). The agent param defaults to caller but is only format-validated — any registered agent can replace or clear any teammate's avatar silently, inconsistent with teammate_update (self-only by schema). Evidence: tools.py:1010-1029 vs 960-971. WP-14 never discussed write-target authorization (not in known-intentional list). See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #T1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Make avatars installable out of the box: images extra path, correct error remediation, README section
data.detail: Fable-audit P2 (high). Pillow is behind the images extra; session-start.sh runs uv sync --no-dev with no --extra images, so no adopter ever has it. The CommsError prescribes "pip install teammate-comms[images]" — impossible (not on PyPI; runtime is a uv plugin-cache venv). README never mentions Pillow/avatars enablement. Fix: env-gated --extra images in the hook (or documented uv command), correct error text, README section. Evidence: hooks/session-start.sh:66, avatars.py:150-158, pyproject.toml:14-15. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #P2.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Un-hardcode reincarnate marketplace spec; surface spawn failure to the caller
data.detail: Fable-audit W1 (high). spawn.py:30-34 pins MARKETPLACE="coltondyck" in PLUGIN_SPEC/allowlists/channel_allowlisted(); any fork/rehost silently breaks reincarnate (child stdio DEVNULL → failure looks identical to success per tools.py:1550-1554). Fix: derive the marketplace/plugin spec (or make configurable), document $TEAMMATE_LAUNCH_ARGS as the escape hatch, and give the caller a post-spawn liveness check/diagnostic. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #W1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Session-scope the TEAMMATE_REINCARNATE_ENABLED opt-in gate
data.detail: Fable-audit W2 (high). The gate is a bare env check (tools.py:1505-1516); setx/profile export — the normal way to persist an env var — silently enables process-spawning machine-wide for every future session. Incident c1fa517c047d already happened; fix was OS cleanup only, no code mitigation. Fix direction: session-scoped confirmation, a freshness/nonce component, or at minimum a visible "reincarnate is enabled" indicator at register. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #W2.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add macOS to the CI matrix
data.detail: Fable-audit P4 (high). Matrix is ubuntu+windows only; spawn.py:42-51 has darwin-specific managed-settings branching and README documents macOS paths — all unexercised. macOS is a large fraction of individual Claude Code users (the audit yardstick). Evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml:20-27. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #P4.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Docs sync: set_avatar + show_all + teammate_list scoping into README/DESIGN; fix DESIGN version framing; release doc-checklist
data.detail: Fable-audit X1+X2+X3+X5+X6 (high). README/SKILL/DESIGN tool tables all miss teammate_set_avatar (18th tool); README never documents default-on inbox body-suppression/show_all (current release headline) nor teammate_list's project-scoping default (contradicts "global by default" framing); DESIGN.md header/plugin.json example still say v0.7.1 (shipped 0.11.0 — second occurrence of this drift, AUDIT E-2/WP-2 fixed it once); WP-14/WP-15 have no DESIGN sections and avatars.py is absent from DESIGN's layout. Root fix: a release checklist enforcing README+SKILL+DESIGN updates in the same PR. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #X1-X3,#X5,#X6.
context: fable-audit X1 X2 X3 X5 X6
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Rewrite SKILL.md reliability contract to the honest WP-9 wording
data.detail: Fable-audit X4 (high). SKILL.md:106-116 still claims "a dropped/missed channel push never loses a message — it is read on the next teammate_inbox" — the exact framing DESIGN.md:265-288 was deliberately rewritten (WP-9 honest contract) to retract: an idle agent never calls inbox unprompted and re-nudge caps at 3 attempts. SKILL.md is the surface actually loaded into adopters' agents, so it carries the superseded guarantee to exactly the wrong audience. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #X4.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Cap unread.json growth like _read.json
data.detail: Fable-audit T2 (normal). _READ_CAP (1000) bounds the acked log (audit C-3 fix) but the live unread queue that send_dm/send_group append to is never capped — a never-acking recipient accumulates an unbounded file parsed on every inbox call and watcher pass. Evidence: tools.py:85-87, 798-806 vs 598-610, 1088-1105. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #T2.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Group message storage: O(1) append (NDJSON) or compaction for groups/*/messages.json
data.detail: Fable-audit C3 (normal). append_group_message does read-full-array → append → rewrite-full-file on every post (comms.py:401-420), and group history is never compacted — post cost scales with total history, unlike every other event store (O(1) NDJSON appends). See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #C3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Cross-host id ordering: document the limit or add a per-host sequence component to message ids
data.detail: Fable-audit C4 (normal). Message ids are naive local timestamps ("writer and reader are always co-located", comms.py:33-35) while DESIGN.md:670-676 documents a supported cross-host shared root; all cursors assume monotonic ids across writers, so clock skew between hosts silently and permanently skips messages. Either document cross-host as unsupported for ordering or add a per-host disambiguator/logical clock. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #C4.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Avatar lifecycle: GC sidecars on teammate removal; registration check in /avatar; base64 length pre-check
data.detail: Fable-audit D2+D3 (normal). remove_teammate never unlinks avatars/<name>.* (comms.py:767-778) and /avatar serves any shape-valid name from disk without checking registration (dashboard.py:219-243) — removed teammates' images stay fetchable and name-reusers inherit stale avatars. Separately, image_base64 is fully b64decoded before the 50MB cap is applied (avatars.py:166-180) — cap the encoded string length first; note stdin JSON-RPC has no message-size cap at all (server.py:340-351). See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #D2,#D3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Dashboard: surface poll/roster failures in the UI (stale token, 401/500, dead server)
data.detail: Fable-audit D4 (normal), sharpened by Stage-2 dashboard-seam finding: poll()/refreshRoster() wrap everything in bare catch(e){} and reschedule unconditionally (index.html:505-529) — a permanent 401 from a stale token (new token minted per server process; old one baked into the tab's JS closure, URL token stripped by history.replaceState at index.html:134) is indistinguishable from a quiet inbox. The tab goes silently, permanently dead after any server restart. Fix: distinguish auth failure from transient error; show a reconnect banner. WP-4 added error surfacing for writes only (41d37ae0b2b2). See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #D4 and the Stage-2 interconnectivity doc.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: spawn.py platform fixes: shlex.split(posix=False) on Windows; reap spawned children
data.detail: Fable-audit W5+W6 (normal). TEAMMATE_LAUNCH_ARGS is parsed with POSIX shlex rules even on Windows — unquoted backslash paths are silently corrupted (spawn.py:94-98), in a "Windows-first" module, on the exact override path a forked-marketplace adopter needs. And every launch path drops its Popen handle with no wait/poll (spawn.py:141-183) — POSIX zombies accumulate for the life of the server. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #W5,#W6.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Watcher robustness: atomic identity snapshot+generation read; act on heartbeat write failures
data.detail: Fable-audit W4+W7 (normal). snapshot() and get_generation() take Identity._lock separately (channel.py:215-216; server.py:73-97) — a set() between them pairs a stale root/inbox with a new generation for one tick (residual WP-12 hole). And run_watcher ignores write_agent_record's False return on lock timeout (channel.py:239-243) — a silently-failed heartbeat can make a live agent look stale to is_channel_alive, which reincarnate's collision guard trusts (tools.py:1524-1529). Fix: one combined snapshot-with-generation accessor; log/retry failed heartbeats. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #W4,#W7.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Test gaps: avatars.py error paths; one true multi-process contention scenario
data.detail: Fable-audit Q4+Q5 (normal). avatars.py failure branches (oversize, invalid base64, corrupt image, zero-dimension, bomb guard — avatars.py:169-194) have zero tests; only happy paths + Pillow-absent are covered. All concurrency coverage is threading-based in one interpreter (test_handshake.py:1265-1276, test_wp13_projects.py:223-226); the product's actual shape — separate processes contending on the same files — is never driven. Add error-path tests and at least one subprocess-based contention test. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #Q4,#Q5.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: reinject-instructions.sh: add defensive self-filter + matcher-contract test (close AUDIT G-4 for real)
data.detail: Fable-audit P5 (normal). AUDIT G-4 flagged the hooks.json matcher behavior as unverified; the fix marked MERGED only gave session-start.sh a stdin self-filter — reinject-instructions.sh (matcher:"compact") has none and no test proves the matcher contract. Evidence: hooks/reinject-instructions.sh (no stdin read), hooks/session-start.sh:11-15, test_handshake.py:1806-1816. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #P5.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Surface $TEAMMATE_AGENT auto-register failure in-conversation
data.detail: Fable-audit S4 (normal). _maybe_auto_register (server.py:255-265) logs CommsError to stderr only; a reincarnate-spawned child with a bad name looks alive to its parent but never registers, and its standing instructions don't distinguish "auto-register silently failed" from "not yet registered". Surface the failure in the instructions text or first tool response. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #S4.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Document the flat-trust authorization model; align the inconsistent tools
data.detail: Fable-audit T3 (normal). teammate_update is self-only by schema; teammate_delete removes any offline teammate globally; project_register/project_delete accept any key with convention-only prose. An adopter cannot predict which tools are safe defaults. Flat local trust is deliberate (d30a0bc88264) — the inconsistency and non-documentation are the gap. Evidence: tools.py:1460-1488, 1611-1647, 1770-1782. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #T3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Transcript/reactions compaction (finish the C-2 deferral)
data.detail: Fable-audit C6 (low). WP-7 gave deletions real compaction; transcript.jsonl and reactions.jsonl only got read-side tail optimization — disk growth is unbounded on the global root. Evidence: comms.py:1301-1397 has no transcript/reactions analog. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #C6.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Re-nudge cap residual: document the manual-recovery affordance for permanently-dropped wakes
data.detail: Fable-audit W3 (low). REEMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 (~14 min) is deliberate (WP-9, 5004a641bd82); after exhaustion a lone message under sustained notification drops is silent forever with no user-visible indicator — only a manual teammate_inbox recovers it. Document the failure mode + recovery (and consider an indicator in teammate_list/whoami for pending un-acked pushes). Evidence: channel.py:49-50, 139-174. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #W3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Negotiate protocolVersion instead of echoing the client's
data.detail: Fable-audit S3 (low). initialize reflects params.get("protocolVersion") back (server.py:227-237) instead of answering with a version the server implements — a false compatibility claim if Claude Code ever ships an incompatible protocol. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #S3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Tag releases in git (v0.7.0 … v0.11.0 and forward)
data.detail: Fable-audit X7 (low). git tag is empty despite 8+ CHANGELOG version headers; adopters cannot check out a release — SHAs must be reverse-engineered from marketplace pin commits. See docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md #X7.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fable Audit 260701 — Stage 2: Interconnectivity audit
data.detail:
Fable Audit Stage 2 (2026-07-01), teammate-comms, seams between systems; yardstick = third-party adopters. 5 Sonnet auditors split by boundary (message lifecycle, identity/liveness, dashboard-store, plugin-harness, cross-project/OS/host). Full text: docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md. Findings:
I1 CRITICAL bug: cross-host live name collision never resolves — is_channel_alive's pid check is host-gated so both hosts see the other alive; field-merge write_agent_record + 5s heartbeats flip record ownership forever; name-keyed inbox means both instances drain one mailbox — silent non-deterministic misdelivery (server.py:180-197, comms.py:860-883, channel.py:238-243).
G1 CRITICAL gap: teammate_list filters by raw project string, never validate_project_key — the one entry point WP-13 normalization skipped, violating WP-13's own acceptance criterion #2; cross-OS teammates silently vanish from the roster while list_projects shows them together (tools.py:815-832 vs 1578-1608, dashboard.py:257-266).
I2 high: reincarnate collision guard can't see a live human (register_human never sets channel → is_channel_alive always False; remove_teammate has the type=="human" carve-out, reincarnate forgot it) — spawned child hijacks operator identity (tools.py:1509-1529 vs 1471-1472).
I3 high bug: remove_agent unlinks with no lock and swallows OSError; teammate_delete always reports success — deletion can silently no-op on Windows (comms.py:767-778, tools.py:1479-1488).
I4 med-high: delete-then-heartbeat resurrects a type-less ghost record via the empty-dict merge path — send_dm then claims "no teammate named X registered" for a live agent (channel.py:238-243, comms.py:801-825).
G2 high: no case-folding for agent/group names — same names merge on Windows, split on Linux, on the same shared root (comms.py:31,74-84).
G3 high: default project key = parent-dir name (server.py:131-134) — same repo cloned at different paths splits rosters by default; WP-8 traded collision risk for unrec

data.summary: Identity ownership & reconciliation: instance-id/epoch in agent records, detect competing claimants
data.detail: Fable-audit I1 (critical) + root fix for I2/I4/B1/G2. Cross-host live name collision never resolves: is_channel_alive's pid check is host-gated, both hosts see the other alive, field-merge writes flip record ownership every ~5s, and the name-keyed inbox is drained by both instances — silent misdelivery. Structural fix: add an instance-id/epoch to agent records, compare-and-swap or detect-on-write, and a defined collision-resolution protocol surfaced in-conversation. Evidence: server.py:180-197, comms.py:860-883, channel.py:238-243. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #I1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Normalize project comparison in teammate_list via validate_project_key
data.detail: Fable-audit G1 (critical). _handle_list compares raw project strings (tools.py:815-816, 830-832) while _resolve_caller_project_key/_derive_project_roster and dashboard.py:257-266 normalize — the one entry point the WP-13 fix skipped, directly violating WP-13's own acceptance criterion #2 (WP-13-project-profiles.md:170-173) and constraint 3a81e56eb341. Cross-OS teammates silently vanish from the default roster view. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #G1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Reincarnate collision guard: add the human/type carve-out remove_teammate already has
data.detail: Fable-audit I2 (high). register_human never sets a channel key, so is_channel_alive(human_record) is always False — teammate_reincarnate's guard (tools.py:1509-1529) sails past a live operator and the spawned child's auto-register merge-writes type="full", channel=True over the human's record: identity hijack. remove_teammate has the type=="human" check (tools.py:1471-1472); reincarnate needs the same. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #I2.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Locked, verified deletion in remove_agent; prevent heartbeat ghost-resurrection
data.detail: Fable-audit I3+I4 (high). remove_agent unlinks with no lock and swallows OSError (comms.py:767-778) while teammate_delete always reports success (tools.py:1479-1488) — on Windows the deletion can silently no-op. And a deleted-but-alive teammate's next watcher heartbeat recreates its record via write_agent_record's empty-dict merge path WITHOUT type (channel.py:238-243, comms.py:801-825) — send_dm then claims "no teammate named X is registered" for a live agent. Fix: lock + verify deletion outcome; make the recreate path refuse to fabricate a type-less record (or have heartbeats include type). See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #I3,#I4.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Human presence staleness: TTL/heartbeat for presence; stop cross-dashboard clobber
data.detail: Fable-audit B1 (high). Human presence is the flat flag the agent-liveness design deliberately rejected (c362e41c838f): set "online" at start_dashboard, flipped "away" only by graceful shutdown (dashboard.py:496-514) — a killed terminal leaves the human online forever to every teammate. Under the default "human" name, two dashboards share one presence field and cross-clobber (one operator's exit marks the other offline). Fix: presence TTL/heartbeat while the dashboard is polled, plus per-dashboard identity. Evidence: comms.py:896-914, dashboard.py:255-256, tools.py:837-841,1565. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #B1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Group fan-out deferred members: retry and/or persisted behind-marker the watcher can wake on
data.detail: Fable-audit M2 (high). send_group's per-member inbox write is best-effort; on lock contention the member goes into deferred and nothing else happens — no retry, no marker, and the watcher only polls _unread.json (never groups/*/messages.json). Only the SENDER is told. The deferred member gets no wake ever: the "no polling" promise silently breaks in group chat under load. Evidence: tools.py:1085-1108, channel.py:192-325. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #M2.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Stabilize default project identity: derive from git remote or warn on cross-machine key divergence
data.detail: Fable-audit G3 (high). The auto-filled project label is literally parent-dir-name/repo-name from cwd (server.py:131-134) — the same repo cloned under ~/Projects vs ~/dev on two machines yields different keys and silently split rosters. WP-8 (35f29d15eccb) introduced this to fix basename collisions and never recorded the split-risk tradeoff. Fix direction: prefer a stable identifier (git remote) when available, or detect/warn when teammates' keys nearly-match. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #G3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Detect comms-root divergence between peers (root fingerprint + diagnostics)
data.detail: Fable-audit G4 (high). Each process resolves its comms root independently; a sender ensure_inbox-es the recipient into its OWN root and reports "queued, teammate offline" — indistinguishable from a real offline peer. One agent with TEAMMATE_COMMS_DIR set and a peer without can never exchange a message and can't diagnose why. Fix: record a root fingerprint, compare in doctor/send paths, surface mismatch hints. Evidence: comms.py:221-250, tools.py:580-617. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #G4.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Agent/group name case-folding policy (merge-vs-split depends on host filesystem today)
data.detail: Fable-audit G2 (high). validate_agent_name accepts mixed case; stores key off literal strings. "Bob"/"bob" silently collapse on Windows (NTFS case-insensitive) and are two invisible-to-each-other identities on Linux sharing the same root. Project keys got case-folding in WP-13; names never did. Fix: fold at compare or reject case-variant collisions at register. Evidence: comms.py:31, 74-84, 194-210, 813. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #G2.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Harness contract guards: version-stamp managed-settings detection; verify initialize.instructions; first-install restart signal
data.detail: Fable-audit H2+H5+H3 (high). (a) channel_allowlisted() reconstructs an undocumented Claude Code managed-settings schema verified once against v2.1.161 (6e017ca575eb) with no version guard — schema drift silently yields a dead spawned teammate under a success message (spawn.py:56-82). (b) The assumption that MCP initialize.instructions reaches the model at all was never verified — the whole standing-instructions contract rests on it. (c) First-install hook-before-spawn ordering isn't guaranteed; the fix ("restart Claude Code") is prose-only with no in-product signal. Add version stamps + runtime mismatch warnings, verify the instructions assumption once, and signal restart-needed in-product. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #H2,#H5,#H3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: TEAMMATE_LAUNCH_ARGS: strip or surface the allowlist-bypass inheritance
data.detail: Fable-audit H4 (normal). If set, TEAMMATE_LAUNCH_ARGS is used verbatim — skipping channel_allowlisted() entirely — and is deliberately NOT stripped from child env, so one stray setx/export overrides trust detection for every descendant reincarnation forever with no whoami visibility. Half-applied lesson of incident c1fa517c047d (the gate var got a strip; this one didn't). Fix: strip, or surface its presence in whoami/reincarnate output + document. Evidence: spawn.py:94-97, 107-138. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #H4.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Mid-session plugin-update drift: version-stamp reinjected instructions; detect server-vs-disk mismatch
data.detail: Fable-audit H1 (normal). The server's INSTRUCTIONS/TOOL_DEFINITIONS freeze at spawn (server.py:25-26); the compact hook re-execs python -m teammate_comms.instructions fresh from disk (reinject-instructions.sh:27-29). A plugin update mid-session means compaction injects NEW instructions describing an OLD running server — defeating the single-source-of-truth design through the file-vs-process split. Fix: include the version in both and warn on mismatch. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #H1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Seed watcher last_seen from durable {agent}_seen.json at register
data.detail: Fable-audit M3 (normal). Identity._last_seen starts None and is only populated by the session's first teammate_inbox call — never from the WP-15 durable seen file. A new message arriving before that first read produces a wake over-counting stale already-shown messages ("3 new" that reads as "1 new, 2 already delivered"). WP-15's T6 test only covers post-first-read behavior. Evidence: channel.py:285-296 vs tools.py:668-701; server.py:67. Respect the None-sentinel design (6dc06fd9c6b6) when fixing. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #M3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: react(): use the same 3-tier id-resolution fallback as resolve_message; error on nonexistent ids
data.detail: Fable-audit M4 (Stage 2) + Stage-3 tool-surface finding (normal). react() resolves ids transcript-only (tools.py:1349-1372) while delete's resolve_message has a 3-tier fallback (transcript → group file → inbox, tools.py:1386-1422) — reaction wakes silently lost with TEAMMATE_TRANSCRIPT=0. Worse for a newcomer: reacting to a NONEXISTENT id returns a confident success string ("Reacted 👍 to message X") while ack/delete both raise actionable id-not-found errors — a typo'd reaction reaches nobody, silently. Unify on resolve_message and error when the id resolves nowhere. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #M4 and the Stage-3 consumer doc.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Distinguish ack-without-read from read in group read-receipts
data.detail: Fable-audit M5 (normal). ack("all") with last_seen=None (cold start) drains the whole unread queue into _read.json; group_read_positions infers read-receipts from _read.json membership and teammate_group(action="reads") presents "caught up" for messages never actually seen. Fix: mark cold-start-drained entries distinctly, or exclude them from read-position inference. Evidence: tools.py:770-788 → comms.py:917-933 → tools.py:1265-1275. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #M5.

data.summary: Message-id uniqueness across writers (per-writer disambiguator; fixes dashboard dedup drop)
data.detail: Fable-audit B3 (normal), extends task e3288a850c64 (cross-host id ordering). Ids are bare microsecond timestamps with no writer disambiguator; the dashboard dedups on one global seen-Set across all conversations/writers (index.html:189-198) — two different messages stamping the same microsecond means one silently never renders though both are durably stored. _window's collision reasoning (comms.py:966-981) covers one writer's window only. Fix alongside the id-scheme work: add a per-writer component to ids. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #B3.

data.summary: Reject Windows reserved device names in agent/group/project validation
data.detail: Fable-audit G5 (normal). con/aux/nul/com1-9/lpt1-9 pass validate_agent_name/validate_project_key but Windows refuses the file/lock paths (con.json, con.json.lock) — failure surfaces as a silently-swallowed lock break or a raw "failed unexpectedly: OSError". Add reserved-name checks to the validators with a clean error. Evidence: comms.py:31, 127, 657-683, 801-825; tools.py:1817-1823. See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #G5.

data.summary: Document supported/unsupported cross-host storage transports (SMB/NFS/sync-folder caveats)
data.detail: Fable-audit G6 (normal). The cross-host shared-root mode's atomicity foundations (os.replace same-volume, mkdir lock exclusivity — comms.py:467-482, 612-647) are unverified on SMB/NFS and actively violated by OneDrive/Dropbox sync semantics (out-of-band materialization, conflicted-copy siblings no code path reads). DESIGN §10 documents the mode without transport caveats. Either verify or scope the documentation; compounds C1 (destructive read) and C4 (clock skew). See docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md #G6.

No notifications/tools/list_changed mechanism exists if the client ever caches tool schemas across a same-session server respawn (server.py:242-243). $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is independently re-expanded by hooks.json, plugin.json, and reinject-instructions.sh at different times with no consistency verification — the shared root under the H1/H2 drift findings. data.summary: Fable Audit 260701 — Stage 3: Consumer audit
data.detail:
Fable Audit Stage 3 (2026-07-01), teammate-comms, consumer/newcomer lens; yardstick = third-party adopters. 3 Sonnet auditors: literal README walkthrough, zero-context agent on the MCP tool surface, advanced-flow consumer (operator/scaler/spawner/troubleshooter/uninstaller). Full text: docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md. Findings:
N1 critical (corroborates P1/P3, task d0e5caeb18fd): first contact = one symptom (no teammate_* tools), three silent causes (no uv, no bash, first-sync timing), no differentiating diagnostic; README never mentions /mcp.
N2 high NEW: the headline flow (two instances register → send → wake) has NO worked example in README, while groups/project-profiles get runnable scripts (README:100-108 vs 227-228).
N3 high NEW: TEAMMATE_REINCARNATE_ENABLED has no safe documented set-command on any OS — a regression: the old setx demo caused incident c1fa517c047d and was removed with no replacement; propagation model (must pre-date Claude Code launch; mid-session export does nothing; durable set = permanent machine-wide) undocumented.
N4 high NEW: no Troubleshooting section anywhere; real diagnostics (/mcp, ~/.claude/debug log, whoami doctor) live only in SKILL.md which README never links; failed-reincarnate diagnosis exists only in that tool's return text; dashboard register-precondition undocumented.
N5: walkthrough confirmed stale docs bite in practice (17-vs-18 tools, show_all, teammate_list scoping — task 2ec3a014b36f; avatars dead-end a908cfa546cc; marketplace hardcode 0fd06ee573ed verified currently-matching via live WebFetch).
N6 medium NEW: group membership is cosmetic — any registered agent can read any group's history; only delete is gated; description invites a Slack privacy model (tools.py:1277-1279, 306-317; join/add openness deliberate 4152caab6808, read-side never weighed).
N7 medium NEW: @mention syntax invisible at schema level — lives only in SKILL.md; WP-11b leanness pass (2bf2ede47d91) swept core behavior out with the prose.
N8 medium

data.detail: Fable-audit N2 (high, Stage 3). The plugin's headline flow — two full instances exchanging a first message — has no worked example anywhere in README, while groups (README:100-108) and project profiles get runnable scripts. Add a copy-pasteable quickstart covering both terminals: register on each, send, what the wake looks like, inbox, ack. See docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md #N2.

data.summary: Document safe per-OS enablement of TEAMMATE_REINCARNATE_ENABLED incl. the propagation model
data.detail: Fable-audit N3 (high, Stage 3). README names the gate but shows no set-command on any OS — a regression: the old setx demo caused incident c1fa517c047d and was removed with no safe replacement. The propagation model is undocumented and counterintuitive: the MCP server forks at Claude Code startup, so a mid-session export does nothing, while setx/profile persistence silently enables spawning machine-wide forever. Document a session-scoped launch pattern per OS + the risks. Complements code task 2e9c79d023e2. Evidence: README.md:184-190, tools.py:1504-1516. See docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md #N3.

data.summary: README Troubleshooting section + README↔SKILL cross-links
data.detail: Fable-audit N4+N1 (high, Stage 3). No troubleshooting path exists for any failure: symptom→cause for first-contact failures (no uv / no bash / first-sync all present as "no teammate_* tools"), the /mcp connection check, ~/.claude/debug/<session-id>.txt, teammate_whoami(verbose) doctor, a failed-reincarnate walkthrough (its candid diagnosis exists only in the tool's own return text, tools.py:1547-1555), dashboard-blank causes (stale token, TEAMMATE_TRANSCRIPT=0, root mismatch), and the dashboard's register precondition (tools.py:1558-1566). data.summary: Document dashboard lifecycle; consider a stable relaunch affordance for daily human use
data.detail: Fable-audit N12 (high, Stage 3). The dashboard mints a fresh token (and possibly port) per hosting server process and dies with the instance — a bookmarked URL 403s after any Claude Code restart, and the human must ask an agent to relaunch and repaste a new link. This lifecycle is documented nowhere; the "human as first-class teammate, daily use" framing implies persistence that doesn't exist. Document it, and consider a stable relaunch story (e.g. fixed port + token file the human can reopen). Evidence: dashboard.py:15-16, 454-493; tools.py:1558-1575. See docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md #N12.

Open join/add is deliberate (4152caab6808, code comment "F-2: open membership is intentional"); the open READ side was never weighed or documented. Either state it in the description or gate reads. data.summary: WP-19 gate catch: unlocked epoch read-back after bump_epoch write reintroduces the I1 flap through the register-race door — return the minted value from under the lock, never read it back
data.detail: Svetlana's WP-19 (080c531) computed epoch race-free under write_agent_record's lock (bump_epoch=True) but then stamped Identity._epoch from an UNLOCKED read_agent_record read-back. Two competitors registering the same name within the window both read back the same final epoch (N+1), both store it as "my epoch", and the TOCTOU tie-break (record.epoch == my_epoch → re-claim) then makes BOTH permanently re-claim: deterministic mutual flap — the exact I1 failure the permit exists to kill, reachable only in the two-claimants case the WP is about. The implementer's inline comment ("nothing else can have changed the record yet") was true single-threaded and false exactly under contention. Fix: write_agent_record returns the merged record dict on success (truthiness-compatible with every existing caller); register consumes epoch + profile-echo from the return value, deleting the read-back. General pattern: a value minted under a lock must LEAVE the critical section by return value, not by a subsequent read — the read-back is a self-inflicted TOCTOU. Caught at the manager diff gate (hunt-list item derived from war-gaming the spec addendum); all three suites were green with the hole present — tests can't see a two-process microsecond window they don't construct.

data.summary: An epic's own just-fixed anti-pattern re-plants itself in the SAME epic's new code — gate every new WP against the epic's already-fixed defect classes, not just against main
data.detail: Twice in one night, new code written to FIX audit findings reintroduced a defect class the same epic had already closed: (1) WP-19 killed the I1 record-flap with a race-free locked epoch, then stamped that epoch via an unlocked read-back — a self-inflicted TOCTOU of the exact shape being fixed; (2) WP-22 built the M2 pending-recovery lane (whose whole purpose is never-lose-a-message) and peeked the multi-writer pending file with the unlocked destructive read_json_safe — the exact C1 anti-pattern WP-17 had fixed on unread.json days earlier IN THE SAME EPIC. Both passed all three green suites; both were caught only at the manager diff gate by checking new code against the epic's OWN fixed-class list (hunt lists carried items like 'destructive read on any multi-writer file' as standing greps). Implication for gate method: maintain a running list of defect classes fixed so far in the epic and re-scan EVERY subsequent diff for each class — the implementer's attention is on the new finding, not on the classes already retired, which is precisely where they resurface. This is the intra-epic sibling of the recorded blind-spot pattern 8def32b8ff63 (when one input path is guarded, audit ALL sibling paths).
